summary: Multinomial coefficient over a u64 slice via tail-recursive `multinomial_loop` carrying (running sum p, accumulated product r), each iteration invoking the full 5-helper `binomial` composition (trailing_zeros → gcd_stein_loop → gcd → multiply_and_divide → binomial_loop) on (p+k[i], k[i]); black-box reuses `binomial_postcondition_gen` and adds three new obstacle layers — (1) **permutation invariance** proved by bridging the algorithm's left-to-right prefix-scan (`mult_prefix`, `sum_prefix`, `denom_prefix`) to a list closed form `mult_list * list_factorial_prod = factorial sum` via the binomial identity `k!·(n-k)!·C(n,k) = n!` (`factorial_nchoose_eq` by structural induction on n,k), with `List.Perm`-preservation of `mult_list` derived from the closed form + perm invariance of `List.sum` and `list_factorial_prod`, and three bridge lemmas (`sum_prefix_eq_take_sum`, `denom_prefix_eq_take_lfp`, `mult_prefix_eq_mult_list`) connecting the index-keyed prefix functions to `(k.val.toList.take i).map UInt64.toNat`; (2) an **overflow-failure existential** `∃ e, multinomial k = RustM.fail e` triggered when `2^64 ≤ sum_prefix k k.val.size`, proved by strong induction on `k.val.size - i.toNat` with case-split over THREE potential failure sources (`+` overflow on `p+k[i]`, binomial-internal failure, `*` overflow on `r*b`) via three dedicated step-fail lemmas (`multinomial_loop_add_fail`, `multinomial_loop_binom_fail`, `multinomial_loop_mul_fail`); (3) **explicit non-divergence reasoning** on the `RustM = Option (Except Error α)` model — atomic `pure_ne_div`/`fail_ne_div`/`bind_ne_div` combinators are chained to prove `multiply_and_divide_not_div`, `binomial_loop_not_div`, `binomial_not_div` (each by strong induction unfolding the partial-operator if-cascades and using IH on the recursive tail), so the failure-existential's case on `multinomial_u64.binomial (p+x) x` can rule out the `none`/`RustM.div` branch by contradiction. Five contract theorems (empty/singleton boundaries by direct loop unfolding, closed-form postcondition `mult_prefix k size`, overflow failure existential, permutation invariance via shared `mult_list_eq` bridge) project from `multinomial_loop_correct` + `multinomial_loop_fails_aux` masters; loop invariant is double — `p.toNat = sum_prefix k i.toNat ∧ r.toNat = mult_prefix k i.toNat` — with monotonicity (`mult_prefix_mono`, `sum_prefix_mono`, `nchoose_factor_le_mult_prefix`) discharging the per-step overflow preconditions from the global `mult_prefix k size < 2^64` fit hypothesis.
